title: Role-Based Access Control (RBAC) Setup
content:
CloudDash RBAC controls what actions team members can perform based on assigned roles. Proper RBAC configuration ensures least-privilege access to monitoring data and configuration.

Built-In Roles: Viewer — read dashboards, view alerts and alert history, access shared reports. Cannot modify configurations or invite users. Editor — all Viewer permissions plus create/edit dashboards, alert rules, integrations, and notification channels. Cannot manage billing, SSO, or team membership. Admin — full organization access including billing, team management, SSO configuration, API key management, and audit log access.

Assigning Roles: Settings > Team Management > select member > Change Role. Role changes take effect immediately. Bulk role assignment available via CSV import on Enterprise plans.

Custom Roles (Enterprise): Create granular roles under Settings > Security > Custom Roles. Define permissions per resource type: dashboards (read, write, delete, share), alerts (read, write, delete, acknowledge), integrations (read, write, delete), settings (read, write), billing (read, write), and audit_logs (read). Assign custom roles alongside or instead of built-in roles.

Resource-Level Permissions: Restrict dashboard access to specific teams using dashboard sharing settings. Set visibility to Organization (all members), Team (specific team only), or Private (creator only). Alert rules inherit visibility from their parent dashboard unless overridden.

API Key Scopes: API keys respect RBAC scopes independently. A Read Only API key owned by a Viewer cannot perform write operations even if the Viewer is promoted to Editor later. Re-create API keys after role changes if scope alignment is needed.

Best Practices: Assign Viewer role by default for new members. Grant Editor only to engineers who manage infrastructure monitoring. Limit Admin to 2-3 trusted individuals. Review role assignments quarterly using the Access Review report (Enterprise). Enable SSO group mapping to automate role assignment based on IdP groups.
